description_long: This obelisk is a tall, narrow, and flat-sided monument with a slightly irregular pentagonal top, resembling a stylized building facade. The front and back faces are adorned with a minimalist line drawing of three human figures in classical attire, rendered in a reddish-brown color. Four small, square, metallic or reflective tiles are vertically aligned along one side of the face, adding a modern geometric accent. The surface appears to be made of a light-colored stone or concrete, with a matte, slightly rough finish. The sides are extremely thin, giving the structure a blade-like profile when viewed from the side. There are no visible inscriptions, but the artistic motif and geometric tiles serve as the primary decorative elements.
description: A tall, thin obelisk with a light stone surface, featuring minimalist line art of three figures and four small square tiles on its face.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full line art of three figures and four square tiles aligned vertically on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the obelisk's extreme thinness, appearing almost as a line.
description_view_2: Back view mirrors the front, displaying the same line art and tile arrangement, with the pentagonal top slanting in the opposite direction.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the obelisk's very thin profile.
attribute_ornamentation: Minimalist line art of three human figures in classical attire on the main face, with four small square metallic or reflective tiles aligned vertically on one side.
attribute_base_design: The base is not distinctly visible, but the obelisk appears to extend straight to the ground with no steps or additional platform, maintaining the same material and finish as the rest of the structure.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and angled, forming an irregular pentagonal shape with no additional decoration or embellishment.
